quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "Gadget led with $2,392.00 (33.4% of revenue) on just 16 units" and "North's total is 53.8% higher than West's."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with figures and an action, e.g. "Reprice Sprocket, which moves the most units yet earns just 15.8% of revenue at $4.75 each."

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like Gadget leading revenue "despite selling the most units" being Sprocket's problem and the inverse price/volume tension across products and regions.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "Gadget led with $2,392.00 (33.4% of revenue)" and "North's total is 53.8% higher than West's."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with figures, e.g. "Reprice Sprocket, which moves the most units yet earns just 15.8% of revenue at $4.75 each."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like Gadget leading on only 16 units due to its $149.50 price and Sprocket selling the most units yet earning the least revenue.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "North's total is 53.8% higher than West's" and "Gadget led with $2,392.00 (33.4% of revenue) on just 16 units".

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with figures and an action, e.g. "Reprice Sprocket, which moves the most units yet earns just 15.8% of revenue at $4.75 each."
